text: Today I'm going to take some breadfruit from the garden and make crispy breadfruit hash brown sticks. So come with me. Breadfruit is a tropical fruit tree that produces unique, starchy green fruits that can be used in many of the same ways that you would use a potato. You can bake, boil, fry, or roast them and you know that they're ready to harvest when they start to have some sap dripping down the fruit. When they're ready to harvest you just want to go ahead and twist them around and give a little tug and they should fall right off. I always have to make sure that I harvest these in time otherwise the squirrels will get to them first. Back in the kitchen the first thing I like to do is cut a little hole where the stem used to be. Then I flip it over and score the bottom of it to form an X. Once it looks like this you know that you're ready to add it to an open flame and start to roast it. As it roasts I like to just carefully rotate it that way every side manages to get a nice char to it. Once it's done it should look something like this and the X should have started to split apart. After letting it cool for a little bit I slice it in half then I cut them into quarters that way it's easier to remove each little bit of the core. After that's done you could just remove the skin and enjoy roasted breadfruit or what I personally prefer is that in salt, pepper and butter mashing that up real good flattening that mixture and then slicing thin strips that you can fry until a crispy golden brown. The end result are crispy breadfruit sticks that are very similar to hash browns in perfect for breakfast. These are absolutely delicious one of my favorite things to do is just dip them in the egg yolk and then go ahead and take a bite. Let me know if you'd give these a try and until next time get outside and see what you can find.
